title: Runtime Sanitizers, Dynamic Analysis, and Language Profiles · Clean-Machine Lab
summary: Add runtime instrumentation to native and managed-language test profiles. The scenario, data, constraints, and failure modes differ from the workshop. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of a sanitizer and dynamic-analysis matrix with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use AddressSanitizer builds to detect memory safety defects in native code.
Use UndefinedBehaviorSanitizer with appropriate recover or trap policy.
Select thread, memory, leak, race, or platform sanitizers according to risk and compatibility.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# Risk-profiled repository gate portfolio
# Each gate needs purpose, command, failure fixture, owner, and suppression policy.

Passing ordinary tests proves memory access is safe. This does not resolve commit to the mission model.

For commit to the mission model, apply Asan without its stated preconditions, omit the a sanitizer and dynamic-analysis matrix trace and counterexample, and treat one plausible output as complete proof. This does not satisfy Asan for a sanitizer and dynamic-analysis matrix.

steps.title: Extend the cumulative artifact
steps.instruction: Append a gates-sanitizer-ci-qvszex: manifest entry with concrete verify plus failure, owner, or repair evidence.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Sanitizer Ci into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
gates-sanitizer-ci-qvszex:
  verify: [replace with a deterministic check]
  failure: [replace with the rejected condition]
  repair: [replace with a local reproduction and correction path]
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.
